chapter_title.ar: بَابُ الْمُؤْمِنِ وَ عَلَامَاتِهِ وَ صِفَاتِهِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 99 –The Momin, and his signs, and his description

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (244/ 9)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (244/ 9)
text: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) الْمُؤْمِنُ كَمِثْلِ شَجَرَةٍ لَا يَتَحَاتُّ وَرَقُهَا فِي شِتَاءٍ وَ لَا صَيْفٍ قَالُوا يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ وَ مَا هِيَ قَالَ النَّخْلَةُ .

en.hubeali: ‘Heasws said: ‘Rasool-Allahsaww said: ‘The Momin is like a tree who leaves neither fall off in the winter nor in the summer’. They said, ‘O Rasool-Allahsaww! And what is it?’ Heasws said: ‘The palm tree’.
en.sarwar: 16. Through the same chain of narrators it is narrated from the Messenger of Allah who has said the following: “A believer is like the tree whose leaves do not fall off in summer or in winter. They asked, ‘O Messenger of Allah, what tree is it?’ He said, ‘It is the palm-tree.’”
